title: Migdal-Eliashberg and SUS-$Y^2$-SYK
abstract: This note addresses a number of subtle issues pertaining to the long-standing problem of strong phonon-like fermion-boson coupling. Among the central topics are the customary Migdal-Eliashberg approximation in the pertinent Schwinger-Dyson gap equation and its solutions. The previously gained insight is assessed by contrasting it against the various (non-)supersymmetric variants of the Yukawa-Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model. Also, some previously discussed (pseudo-)holographic aspects of fermion pairing in such models are commented upon.
summary: This theoretical note tackles the complex physics of strong electron-boson coupling, bridging established theories like Migdal-Eliashberg superconductivity with modern models of quantum chaos, such as the SYK model. It develops sophisticated mathematical tools to analyze the self-energy and pairing mechanisms in these strongly correlated regimes. The work is significant for its attempt to unify descriptions of high-Tc superconductivity and quantum criticality using advanced field-theoretic techniques.
main_problem: The paper addresses subtle issues in strong phonon-like fermion-boson coupling and analyzes self-energy and gap functions in strongly correlated systems, extending concepts to supersymmetric generalizations of the SYK model.
main_result: The analysis provides simplified, coupled equations for the self-energy and gap function, showing how the ME approximation simplifies the Dyson-Schwinger equations, and establishes specific constraints for the SUSY-SYK model.
method: The work employs advanced theoretical techniques including the Schwinger-Dyson equations, Migdal-Eliashberg approximations, algebraic ansatz solutions, and analysis using conformal field theory dimensions.
model_or_system: The primary models are electron-phonon coupled systems (relevant for superconductivity) and the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK) model, including its Yukawa and supersymmetric generalizations (YSYK, SUSY-SYK).
key_observables: Self-energy $\Sigma(\omega)$, pairing function $\Phi(\omega)$, condensation energy ($\delta F$), and the pairing susceptibility.
important_parameters: Coupling constants ($g$), frequency ($\omega$), and various dimensional exponents ($\Delta_\psi, \Delta_\phi$).
main_assumptions: The analysis frequently relies on the Migdal-Eliashberg approximation (neglecting vertex corrections) and the large-N limit for simplification.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the framework for electron-phonon coupling via the ME approximation, then generalizing the self-energy analysis to NFL behavior using SYK/YSYK models, and finally applying these concepts to the highly constrained framework of SUSY-SYK models.
why_interesting: The deep connection between superconductivity (Eliashberg theory) and quantum chaos models (SYK) provides a powerful, non-trivial theoretical lens for understanding emergent quantum phases in strongly correlated materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
